thesis: TPL combines capital-free Permian royalty interests, surface rights, produced-water royalties, operated water services, and emerging infrastructure options. The hard-to-vary claim is that shareholder value depends on valuing each non-overlapping economic claim after its required capital and realization risk, not on applying a single acreage multiple.

explanation_contracts.mechanism: Perpetual royalty ownership creates capital-free participation in third-party development, but only acreage not represented in the producing run-rate can be added as undeveloped inventory.
explanation_contracts.distinguishing_test: A tract/county inventory must reconcile total NRA to producing and undeveloped buckets.
explanation_contracts.counter_explanation: A high-quality royalty portfolio may still be fully reflected in current cash flow and price.
explanation_contracts.falsifier: Any inventory bridge that overlaps producing cash flow or cannot reconcile to the 224,000 total NRA.

explanation_contracts.mechanism: Produced-water royalties and operated water services have different capital intensity and must be valued separately.
explanation_contracts.distinguishing_test: A primary volume-price-margin-capital bridge must reconcile both streams without infrastructure double counting.
explanation_contracts.counter_explanation: Reported segment growth may conceal capital-consuming service economics.
explanation_contracts.falsifier: Failure to reconcile royalty and service cash flows or required capital.

explanation_contracts.mechanism: Bolt and desalination can add value only as milestone-gated claims net of TPL capital.
explanation_contracts.distinguishing_test: Executed customer terms, ownership, probability, timing, and remaining capital must support the option tree.
explanation_contracts.counter_explanation: Announcements and development activity may never become shareholder cash.
explanation_contracts.falsifier: Loss of anchor customer, failure of Phase 2B economics, or remaining capital materially above modeled amounts.

open_questions: Map the 224,000 total NRA into producing, visible undeveloped, and dormant non-overlapping inventory.
Reconcile produced-water royalties and operated water services by volume, price, margin, maintenance capital, growth capital, and ownership.
Obtain enforceable customer, ownership, remaining-capital, and milestone evidence for Bolt and desalination.
